citations.notes: Facts with identical concept QName, context, and unit (when applicable) represent duplicate assertions and create semantic ambiguity.
Duplicate facts may be rejected under strict validation modes as they violate XBRL semantic consistency.
When duplicate facts exist with different values, this creates direct semantic contradiction in the XBRL instance.
Extension concepts must have anchoring relationships to standard taxonomy concepts. Abstract concepts cannot serve as valid anchor targets.
Extension concepts must anchor to standard taxonomy concepts, not to other extension concepts.
Extension concepts must have consistent periodType with their anchor targets. Duration concepts cannot anchor to instant concepts.
Extension concepts must have compatible data types with their anchor targets.
All extension concepts used in facts must have proper anchoring relationships to standard taxonomy concepts.
Facts cannot specify both decimals and precision attributes simultaneously.
Decimals attribute must be a valid integer or 'INF'.
Precision attribute must be a positive integer.
Numeric facts must specify a unitRef attribute referencing a declared unit.
Non-numeric facts cannot specify unitRef, decimals, or precision attributes.
Facts must use units compatible with their concept data types (e.g., shares concepts require shares units, not currency).
Filings must use consistent taxonomy versions throughout. Mixing different versions of the same taxonomy within a filing creates validation conflicts.
Schema references (schemaRef href) must match the namespaces actually used in facts and contexts.

rules.citation: XEW-P008 local methodology: deterministic registered-instrument identity collapse
rules.notes: P008 is an XEW methodology check, not an EDGAR validity rule. It reports cases where multiple deterministic registered-instrument signatures share the same weak ticker/exchange identity facts and therefore require instrument-level review.
rules.title: CMD+RVL XEW-P008 Instrument Identity Collapse methodology

rules.citation: XEW-P008 local methodology: registry snapshot boundary
rules.notes: XEW consumes only local registry snapshots for P008. Missing, ambiguous, invalid, or absent registry rows must be reported explicitly and must never trigger a live provider lookup or guessed FIGI.
rules.title: CMD+RVL XEW-P008 registry snapshot consumer contract

citations.notes: XBRL instances should not contain duplicate facts with identical concept/context/unit as this creates structural ambiguity in the data model.
Validator tightening may surface duplicate fact ambiguities as fatal errors. Filings should avoid multiple facts with identical concept/context/unit combinations.
When duplicate facts have different values, the contradiction makes the filing data internally inconsistent and unreliable.

rules.citation: XEW-P009 local methodology: identifier basis transition
rules.notes: P009 reports a transition only when deterministic evidence proves continuity across N-PORT periods and the strongest reported basis changes. Strong proof may come from exact identifier continuity, the same observation carrying multiple strong identifiers, or a local canon/OpenFIGI registry snapshot. Weak title, ticker, issuer, or economic similarity alone is not continuity proof.
rules.title: CMD+RVL XEW-P009 Temporal Instrument Identity Drift methodology

rules.citation: XEW-P009 local methodology: strong identifier removed
rules.notes: P009 reports strong identifier removal when a previously reported strong identifier such as CUSIP, ISIN, FIGI, or typed strong identifier is absent in a later N-PORT observation for the same candidate chain. The detector must preserve unresolved status unless another strong basis or local registry bridge proves continuity.
rules.title: CMD+RVL XEW-P009 strong-identifier removal contract

rules.citation: XEW-P009 local methodology: registry bridge available
rules.notes: P009 may treat continuity as registry-bridged only when a local registry snapshot deterministically maps reported identifiers to the same canonical instrument. Pack-time detection must not call OpenFIGI, canon, twinning, HTTP, or model runtimes.
rules.title: CMD+RVL XEW-P009 local registry snapshot boundary

rules.citation: XEW-P009 local methodology: registry bridge missing
rules.notes: P009 reports a missing bridge when continuity would require registry evidence but the supplied local snapshot has no usable row. Missing registry evidence is emitted explicitly and must not be replaced by guessed FIGI, fuzzy matching, or live provider lookup.
rules.title: CMD+RVL XEW-P009 missing registry bridge contract

rules.citation: XEW-P009 local methodology: registry bridge ambiguous
rules.notes: P009 reports an ambiguous bridge when local registry evidence returns multiple non-identical candidate instruments. Ambiguity is review-grade evidence and must be preserved with sorted candidates rather than silently selecting a winner.
rules.title: CMD+RVL XEW-P009 ambiguous registry bridge contract

rules.citation: XEW-P009 local methodology: weak continuity only
rules.notes: P009 reports weak continuity only when observations can be associated solely through weak descriptive evidence such as issuer, title, ticker, value, or economic facts. Weak evidence cannot create a resolved canonical instrument identity and always requires human review.
rules.title: CMD+RVL XEW-P009 weak continuity non-collapse contract

rules.citation: XEW-P009 local methodology: weak-key temporal collision
rules.notes: P009 reports a weak-key temporal collision when the same weak key maps to more than one strong identity over the history window. The detector must emit the collision and avoid collapsing the chain into one canonical instrument.
rules.title: CMD+RVL XEW-P009 weak-key temporal collision contract

citations.citation: Adaptation Marker M002: Extension Refactor Detection
citations.notes: Detects high churn (>30% change) in extension concept set between filings to signal taxonomy rebuilds. Evidence includes counts of new/retired extension QNames and example concepts.
